ucs.title: [LLM] APT39 (Chafer) Plink reverse SSH tunnel for RDP pivot via SOCKS / port-forward
ucs.description: Hunts APT39's signature use of PuTTY's `plink.exe` to establish reverse SSH tunnels (-R), local port forwards (-L) and dynamic SOCKS proxies (-D) into compromised telco/travel networks, which they then use to relay RDP (T1021.001) through the tunnel. FireEye and the FBI specifically called out Chafer/Rana Intelligence Computing using plink + custom SOCKS to bridge their MOIS infrastructure into victim estates.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.dest) as host from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="plink.exe" OR Processes.original_file_name="Plink" OR Processes.process="*plink*") AND (Processes.process="* -R *" OR Processes.process="* -L *" OR Processes.process="* -D *" OR Processes.process="* -pw *" OR Processes.process="* -N *" OR Processes.process="*:22 *" OR Processes.process="*:3389*" OR Processes.process="* -ssh *") AND NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename firstTime as firstTime lastTime as lastTime | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
// APT39 (Chafer) — Plink reverse SSH tunnel / SOCKS proxy / port-forward
// FireEye APT39 Jan 2019; FBI 2020 Rana Intelligence Computing flash
let _lookback = 30d;
let _tunnel_flags = dynamic([" -R ", " -L ", " -D ", " -pw ", " -N ", " -ssh ", ":3389", ":22 ", ":445"]);
let _plink_aliases = dynamic(["plink.exe","plink_x86.exe","plink64.exe","pl.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(_lookback)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"                                  // skip machine accounts
| where FileName in~ (_plink_aliases)
   or ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName =~ "Plink"               // detect renamed plink
   or (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_plink_aliases))
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (_tunnel_flags)
   or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-(R|L|D)\s+\d{2,5}"
| extend TunnelMode = case(
    ProcessCommandLine has " -R ", "reverse-port-forward",
    ProcessCommandLine has " -L ", "local-port-forward",
    ProcessCommandLine has " -D ", "dynamic-SOCKS",
    "ssh-cleartext")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain,
          TunnelMode,
          FileName, FolderPath, SHA256,
          ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT39 (Chafer) — Plink tunnel detection on Sentinel via 4688 / Sysmon-1
let _lookback = 30d;
let _tunnel_flags = dynamic([" -R ", " -L ", " -D ", " -pw ", " -N ", " -ssh ", ":3389", ":22 ", ":445"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(_lookback)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\plink.exe"
   or NewProcessName endswith "\\plink64.exe"
   or NewProcessName endswith "\\plink_x86.exe"
   or ParentProcessName endswith "\\plink.exe"
| where CommandLine has_any (_tunnel_flags)
   or CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-(R|L|D)\s+\d{2,5}"
| extend TunnelMode = case(
    CommandLine has " -R ", "reverse-port-forward",
    CommandLine has " -L ", "local-port-forward",
    CommandLine has " -D ", "dynamic-SOCKS",
    "ssh-cleartext")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, TunnelMode,
          NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT39 Chafer Plink Reverse SSH Tunnel / SOCKS Proxy
id: 7d3a9c20-3f4e-4f1b-9a82-apt39-plink-001
status: experimental
description: Detects plink.exe (PuTTY link) being launched with reverse port-forward, local port-forward, dynamic SOCKS, or cleartext password flags as used by APT39/Chafer/Remix Kitten (Iranian MOIS / Rana Intelligence Computing) for RDP-over-SSH pivoting in telco and travel intrusions.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0087/
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/apt39-iranian-cyber-espionage-group-focused-on-personal-information
  - https://www.fbi.gov/news/press-releases/treasury-sanctions-iranian-cyber-actors-for-attempting-to-influence-the-2020-us-presidential-election
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/04
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1572
  - attack.t1090.001
  - attack.t1090.002
  - attack.lateral_movement
  - attack.t1021.001
  - attack.g0087
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    - Image|endswith:
        - '\plink.exe'
        - '\plink64.exe'
        - '\plink_x86.exe'
    - OriginalFileName: 'Plink'
  selection_flags:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -R '
      - ' -L '
      - ' -D '
      - ' -pw '
      - ' -N '
      - ':3389'
      - ':22 '
      - ':445'
  filter_machine_account:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_image and selection_flags and not filter_machine_account
falsepositives:
  - Authorised network engineers tunnelling through jump hosts (whitelist by user/host)
level: high
ucs.rationale: APT39 is one of the few state-sponsored crews documented (Mandiant 2019, FBI 2020 Rana Intelligence Computing flash) running plink.exe inside victim networks specifically to bridge MOIS C2 to RDP-accessible internal targets in telco/travel/hospitality. Detecting plink+tunnel-flags catches the very stage of their kill chain that turns a single shelled host into estate-wide pivot capability — generi

_field_issues.message: `IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `InitiatingProcessTokenElevation`?

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] APT39 ASPXSpy / China Chopper web shell on Exchange OWA spawning recon LOLBins
ucs.description: Hunts APT39's recurring foothold pattern: drop an ASPXSpy / ASPX web shell into an Outlook Web Access / OWA virtual directory after a public-facing-app exploit (T1190), then drive recon via the IIS worker (`w3wp.exe` under MSExchangeOWAAppPool) spawning cmd/PowerShell/whoami/net/quser. Mandiant and Symantec (Chafer Dec 2015) tied this exact chain to APT39 telco intrusions.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_name) as child values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" AND (Processes.parent_process="*MSExchangeOWAAppPool*" OR Processes.parent_process="*MSExchangeServicesAppPool*" OR Processes.parent_process="*MSExchangeECPAppPool*" OR Processes.parent_process="*DefaultAppPool*") AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","query.exe","hostname.exe","nltest.exe","arp.exe","netstat.exe","reg.exe","wmic.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
// APT39 (Chafer / Remix Kitten) — OWA / Exchange w3wp.exe spawning recon LOLBins
// Symantec Chafer Dec 2015; FireEye APT39 Jan 2019; FBI Rana Intelligence Computing 2020
let _lookback = 14d;
let _recon_lolbins = dynamic([
    "cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe",
    "whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe",
    "tasklist.exe","quser.exe","query.exe","hostname.exe","nltest.exe",
    "arp.exe","netstat.exe","reg.exe","wmic.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe"]);
let _exchange_app_pools = dynamic([
    "MSExchangeOWAAppPool","MSExchangeServicesAppPool",
    "MSExchangeECPAppPool","MSExchangeAutodiscoverAppPool",
    "MSExchangeMapiFrontEndAppPool","MSExchangeRpcProxyAppPool"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(_lookback)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
| where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any (_exchange_app_pools)
| where FileName in~ (_recon_lolbins)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
   or AccountName in~ ("system","network service","iusr")            // OWA pool runs as NT AUTHORITY\NETWORK SERVICE — keep these in scope
| project Timestamp, DeviceName,
          AppPool = extract(@"-ap\s+\"?([^\"\s]+)\"?", 1, InitiatingProcessCommandLine),
          AccountName, AccountDomain,
          ChildImage = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd   = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildHash  = SHA256
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(_lookback)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified")
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\inetpub\wwwroot\", @"\Exchange Server\", @"\ClientAccess\Owa\", @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\")
    | where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx"
    | summarize WebShellCandidates = make_set(strcat(FolderPath, FileName), 25) by DeviceName
  ) on DeviceName
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT39 — Exchange OWA web-shell command exec via w3wp child process
let _lookback = 14d;
let _recon_lolbins = dynamic([
    "cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe",
    "whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe",
    "tasklist.exe","quser.exe","query.exe","hostname.exe","nltest.exe",
    "arp.exe","netstat.exe","reg.exe","wmic.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe"]);
let _exchange_app_pools = dynamic([
    "MSExchangeOWAAppPool","MSExchangeServicesAppPool",
    "MSExchangeECPAppPool","MSExchangeAutodiscoverAppPool",
    "MSExchangeMapiFrontEndAppPool","MSExchangeRpcProxyAppPool"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(_lookback)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
| where ProcessName has_any (_exchange_app_pools) or ParentProcessName has "w3wp"
// 4688 carries parent CommandLine only on newer Windows builds; fall back to NewProcessName check
| extend ChildLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ChildLeaf in~ (_recon_lolbins)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, AccountDomain,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT39 Chafer OWA / Exchange w3wp Spawning Recon LOLBins
id: 4f1c8b62-9a14-4c3a-bd2b-apt39-owashell-001
status: experimental
description: Detects child processes spawned by the IIS worker process w3wp.exe under an Exchange / OWA application pool, where the child is a recon-stage LOLBin (whoami, net, systeminfo, quser, nltest, etc.) — characteristic of APT39 / Chafer ASPXSpy web-shell post-exploitation against Outlook Web Access in telco and travel-sector intrusions.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0087/
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/apt39-iranian-cyber-espionage-group-focused-on-personal-information
  - https://symantec-enterprise-blogs.security.com/blogs/threat-intelligence/iran-based-attackers-use-back-door-threats-spy-middle-eastern-targets
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/04
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.discovery
  - attack.t1033
  - attack.t1018
  - attack.g0087
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  selection_apppool:
    ParentCommandLine|contains:
      - 'MSExchangeOWAAppPool'
      - 'MSExchangeServicesAppPool'
      - 'MSExchangeECPAppPool'
      - 'MSExchangeAutodiscoverAppPool'
      - 'MSExchangeMapiFrontEndAppPool'
      - 'MSExchangeRpcProxyAppPool'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\query.exe'
      - '\hostname.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\arp.exe'
      - '\netstat.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_apppool and selection_child
falsepositives:
  - Exchange health-monitoring scripts run under MSExchange app pools (allowlist by hash / specific cmdlines)
  - Legitimate admin troubleshooting via OWA management shell (rare, should still be reviewed)
level: high
ucs.rationale: APT39's Symantec/FireEye/FBI-documented intrusion lifecycle starts with an internet-facing OWA exploit, then an ASPX web shell (ASPXSpy variant or China Chopper-style) dropped under the OWA virtual directory, then immediate recon via w3wp-spawned LOLBins to enumerate the domain before pivoting to Mimikatz/ProcDump LSASS dumps and RDP. Pinning the parent to `w3wp.exe` *and* an Exchange app pool (ra

_field_issues.message: `FileCreated` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `FileName`?
`FileModified` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `FileSize`?
`Exchange` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Server` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`ClientAccess` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Owa` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`FrontEnd` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`HttpProxy` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
